player: The road is gone. Just forest now.
Foot-wide deer trail. No drone coverage in the canopy.
This is good. This is also terrifying.
Three drones. They haven't spotted us.
maya: We wait, or we go wide left through the switchbacks.

player: Not a drone. Too quiet. Too deliberate.

elias: You're loud.
For what it's worth, you're the first loud thing I've heard in a week that wasn't a machine.
player: We're heading for Ridge Camp. You know it?

elias: Know it. Built half of it.
Name's Elias. I'll walk with you.
Try to keep up.
These trails run east from Shenandoah all the way to Cherokee.
The drones don't come into the thick stuff. Too many obstacles. Bad data.
The mountains don't cooperate with their optimization.
maya: Dense canopy defeats their LIDAR grid.

elias: I spent forty years hunting these hills.
Didn't think of it either. Just noticed it.
White-tail. Buck. Moving north.
First one I've seen this side of the ridge in a month.
Good sign.

player: Someone lived here. Not long ago.
elias: Pettifer family. Three kids.
They went north. Safer, they figured.
Could be right.
Ridge Camp. Keep your hands visible.
They're cautious, not unfriendly. Usually.
Mining bot.
Big one.
maya: They must have extended the extraction zone.

system: The Excavator's rotary drill carves through a boulder like paper.
